title: Smoky Tender Uruguayan Beef Stew
description: A savory, smoky stew that brings Uruguayan flavors to a weeknight dinner, featuring redcurrants and bhuna spice for a unique twist.

ingredients.name: finishing garnish
ingredients.amount: 2 tbsp chopped parsley + 2 tbsp lemon juice

instructions: Pat the beef cubes dry with paper towels and toss them in flour until lightly coated.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat; add the beef in batches and sear until browned on all sides. Transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining oil, onion, and garlic to the pot; sauté until onion is translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in bhuna paste and smoked paprika, cooking for 1 minute to bloom the spices.
Return the beef to the pot, pour in beef broth and tomato puree, then add redcurrants, salt, and pepper. Bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is fork‑tender.
If a thicker gravy is desired, whisk in a cornstarch slurry (1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water) and stir until the sauce thickens, about 2 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, stir in the parsley‑lemon garnish, taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot over mashed potatoes or crusty bread.
